observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to
reflect the correct balances of the Cash and Cash Equivalents and
Liabilities accounts and moving forward, properly record reciprocal
transactions to fairly present the assets and liabilities of the LGU at any
given period;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously prepare monthly bank
reconciliation statements and submit the same to the Office of the
Auditor for review and verification in order to establish the accuracy of
the Cash in Bank balances; and
c. Require the periodic reconciliation of the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to prevent accumulation of unrecorded transactions
and/or errors recording.

We recommended and Management agreed that the municipal accountant
initiate the conduct of cleansing of dormant receivables following strictly
the prescribed procedures in COA Circular 2023-008 to come up with more
reliable balances in the financial statements.

3.11 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit remittances promptly and intact.

3.12 We also recommended and Management agreed to design control to
prevent staggered deposit of collections like, but not limited to, requiring
daily report of collections and deposits from the Municipal Treasurer to
promptly check undeposited remittances during the day or on the next
banking day.

We recommended to the Management the following:
a. Issue Notices to Terminate immediately to the defaulting contractors;
b. Follow strictly the procedures of the termination of contracts
prescribed under Item IV of Annex I of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
c. File for the forfeiture of the advance surety bond posted by the two (2)
contractors who failed to start construction after their contracts had
been terminated; and
d. Impose appropriate sanctions to the defaulting contractors.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to strictly comply with
the provisions of R.A. 9184 in the conduct of public bidding and awarding
of contracts/projects to prevent awarding of contracts to non-eligible bidders.

6.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. provide a safe space for the proper safekeeping of the fuel portable
pump dispenser;
b. establish first the necessity of procurement before approval of
Purchase Requests; and
c. henceforth, conduct proper planning prior to any procurement to
prevent the accumulation of idle government properties.

7.7 We recommended that the LGU:
a. Strengthen coordination with the barangays and strict actual validation
of recipient to ensure that inputs are based on current and verified
needs.
b. Establish a system for periodic review and realignment of procurement
plans in response to actual field data.
c. Implement controls to monitor inventory levels and prevent
overstocking.

8.6 We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the prompt
submission of copies of perfected contracts in accordance with Section
3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to allow sufficient time for proper review
and evaluation.

9.10 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GFPS TWG
Chairperson to:
a. provide the details and composition of the actual costs of the client-
focused and organization-focused activities reported in the GAD
Accomplishment Report in the aggregate amount of ₱14,861,103.03;
and
b. ensure that the GAD Plan, Budget, and Accomplishment Reports are
supported with pertinent documents and other means of verification.

11.12 We recommended and Management agreed to require:
a. the AAO to prioritize the updating of employees' information needed for
GSIS eBCS for immediate remittance of the GSIS premiums that were
not remitted as of December 31, 2024;
b. the concerned employees to refund the amount of loans remitted by the
Municipality and submit the official receipts for evaluation of the audit
team;
c. the monthly reconciliation of the amount withheld and the amount
remitted for every employee to avoid under/over-remittance; and
d. the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to identify the prior years’
unremitted balance of ₱268,221.12 and unreconciled prior year
balances of ₱276,498.08, verify with the GSIS if the LGU has
outstanding premium obligations in prior years, and after which,
coordinate with the designated Electronic File Remittance (ERF)
handler for the reconciliation of the accounts.

14.5 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce settlement
of suspension and disallowances pursuant to Section 7 of 2009 RRSA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Conduct physical count of inventories and prepare a Report thereon;
Diligently prepare, maintain and update their Inventory Records, Forms and Reports, consequently reconcile the same; and
Make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, for the overstatement of inventory.

We recommended and management agreed that the municipal accounting office be vigilant regarding accounts that have abnormal or negative balances and conduct a thorough investigation into the underlying cause. Additionally, we suggest that the accounting and treasury office reconcile their accounts on a regular basis to ensure that their statements reflect the accurate balance.
We further recommended and management agreed that the municipal accountant make the corresponding adjusting entry to record the subject collections.
Management also informed the audit team that through the accounting office they will device a mechanism that SEF and General Fund collections and deposits will be accurately recorded in a separate books of accounts. Further, the treasury office commits to transfer the SEF collections to the SEF books in a monthly basis beginning CY 2024 onwards.

We recommended and management agreed that the Accounting Office effect the necessary adjustments and to submit the said disbursement voucher pertaining to the unaccounted and un-submitted disbursement of ₱2,000.00, duly supported with valid documentation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer verify the report of collections and deposit (RCD) and implement the rules and regulations on collections and remittance.
We further recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of allowing the use of accountable forms by personnel other than those who are allowed by law.
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit collections intact promptly.

We recommended that management implement measures to restrict access to the program of works and detailed estimates to authorized personnel at every stage, beginning with their preparation, continuing through its approval, and concluding with its posting.
Furthermore, we recommended that management request a Rebound Hammer Test at DWPH Apayao First District to ensure the quality of the said projects, and if found substandard and deemed necessary, require the contractor to remove the same.
We recommended and management agreed that NO CASH ADVANCE BE GRANTED TO ELECTED OFFICIALS for purposes other than their official travelling expenses.

We recommended and management agreed to follow strictly the rules on termination of contracts outlined under Annex I of the IRR of RA 9184.
We further recommended and management agreed to blacklist the subject contractors, if necessary.

We recommended and management agreed that:
During the planning stage or before any procurement planning procedures, management secure their rights through deed of donation or deed of sale as the case maybe, to implement projects on private properties to avoid conflicts during project implementation.
To prevent any potential delays, project engineer must closely monitor the timely execution of the project. Should such a circumstance arise, contracts must be terminated following the procedures outlined in the IRR of RA 9184, and if necessary sanctions should be imposed on the contractor.
We recommended and management agreed that the contract manager require the posting of the Performance Security before contracts are being signed and perfected.

We recommended and management agreed to:
Device a strategy for the mandatory collection of the delinquent business tax including its surcharges and interest and yearly payment thereafter;
Require the BPLO in the assessment of the tax to properly check the gross sales/ receipts declared by the taxpayer which are the basis for tax assessment to avoid understatement of tax due.
Management informed the audit team that through the Treasury Office they will inquire to the BLGF for clarification and legal interpretation on the situs of taxation as embodied in the Municipal Revenue Code.

We recommended and management agreed to guarantee the efficient and effective planning and execution of the projects, as well as the optimal utilization of government properties to achieve its objectives.
Management also informed the audit team that they will notify the contractor for the minimal repair considering that this is within the warranty certificate of six (6) months. As for the utilization, management conducted a consultation with the stakeholder particularly with the Sangguniang Bayan Member for the funding of an initial capital of ₱100,000.00 for the initial operation and for the CY 2024; additional funds will be infused.
We recommended and management agreed not issue cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding advances and to stop using cash advances as liquidation.

We recommended and management agreed that project engineers and the inspectorate team be more stringent in their supervision and/or inspection during project implementation and project inspection, respectively, to ensure that government projects are satisfactorily implemented.
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer devise a more effective system that would ensure the withholding of claims of persons liable in the COA Order of Execution.
We recommended and management agreed to stop using the stockpile for basic emergency supplies in their other programs and ensure that they have a ready stockpile in cases of emergency.
We recommended and management agreed to strictly enforce settlement of suspension and disallowances pursuant to Section 7 of 2009 RRSA.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the Accounts Payables, Due to Officers and Employees, Other Payables and Accumulated Surplus/ (Deficit) accounts.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the erroneously recorded expense to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
We recommended management to the immediate repair of the damaged floor to ensure the safety of the health workers and their visiting patients or clients.
We recommended that management allow professional drivers only to handle municipal vehicles.

2.6 We recommended the following:
a. that management establish a system of procedures in the proper accounting and monitoring of received and procured goods and the distribution thereof.
b. that management use the perpetual inventory method in the recording of stocks and conduct an actual physical count of inventory semi-annually and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

3.6 We recommended the following:
a. Blacklist the contractors in accordance with Section 65 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184
b. The BAC to be more judicious in carrying out all aspects of the procurement to ensure that only qualified bidders are selected and to avoid the risk of the procurement process being compromised, especially in the post-qualification stage.

4.5 We recommended that management:
a. direct the Inspectorate Team to strictly assess projects before certifying the satisfactory completion of the same to ensure the proper implementation of projects.
b. take appropriate action on the deliberate lapses of the Inspectorate Team.

5.5 We recommended that the Project Engineers and Municipal Inspectorate Team refrain from asserting information that would deter the imposition of liquidated damages or payment of not yet accomplished scope of works.

5.6 We also recommended for the computation and collection of the un-imposed liquidated damages of these projects.

6.6 We recommended that management require the donation of the incorporated engineering facilities from the contractors; if the asset is already with the municipal government; require the Project Engineer and/or any personnel of the municipality in possession thereof to present the same for proper accounting/recording.

7.6 We recommended that management:
a. adhere strictly to Section 10 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure transparency and competitiveness in procurement transactions;
b. ensure that the APP of the agency contain necessary information that reflects the entirety of the procurement activities that will be undertaken within the calendar year; and
c. resort to alternative modes of procurement only in highly exceptional cases provided for in RA 9184 and see to it that the same will not constitute splitting of contracts.
8.5 We recommended that the management secure ownership of the lot first before starting construction thereon to avoid conflict.

9.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Install/ adopt control measures for the timely procurement of goods taking into consideration the condition and capacity of storage area.
b. Install control in the proper recording and monitoring of all procured goods and the distribution thereof.
10.3 We recommended that management be cautious in the distribution of relief packs to the qualified and rightful beneficiaries to fully attain the objective of the program or project.

11.8 We recommended that Management review the current practices on hiring of Contract of Service (CoS)/ Job Orders to avoid duplication of work and avoid unnecessary expenses to conserve the limited resources of the municipality.

12.6 We recommended that management:
a. strictly implement the “No segregation, No collection” policy
b. strengthens the Information, Education and Communication program on proper waste disposal and ecological solid waste management program of the LGU.
